providers.name: Provider name

providers.modelDescriptionPlaceholder: e.g., Multimodal model with vision capabilities
providers.forbiddenDeveloperSettings: You don't have permission to modify provider configuration.

providerOptions.modelLevelDescription: Free-form JSON merged on top of the provider-level defaults and forwarded as extra request body fields. The shape mirrors the upstream API and differs per provider — Tale forwards verbatim without interpretation.
providerOptions.modelLevelHelp:
Examples — gateways and direct vendors expose different kinds of knobs:
• OpenRouter (gateway): { "provider": { "quantizations": ["fp8"] } } — pin a backend by quantization
• Vercel AI Gateway (gateway): { "metadata": { "tale_agent": "support" } } — observability tag (most routing is header- or model-prefix-based)
• OpenAI (direct): { "service_tier": "priority" } — pick an SLA tier
• Together AI (direct): { "safety_model": "meta-llama/Llama-Guard-4-12B" } — apply moderation

Direct vendors don't expose quantization as a request field — pick a different model ID instead. Keys like model / messages / max_tokens / temperature are rejected — set those at the agent level. Empty input clears the override.
providerOptions.invalidJson: Provider options is not valid JSON
providerOptions.discardConfirmDescription: Your provider-options edits will be lost.
providerOptions.objectRequiredError: Provider options must be a JSON object.

providers.versionConflictDescription: Reload the page to see the latest version, then re-apply your changes.
providers.overwriteEncryptedNoKeyDescription: The provider's secrets file at {path} is SOPS-encrypted, but no decryption key is configured. Saving will discard the existing ciphertext — you cannot recover the previous key from it. Continue?
providers.overwriteUndecryptableDescription: The provider's secrets file at {path} could not be read ({reason}). Saving will discard the existing file — you cannot recover the previous key from it. Continue?
providers.encryptedNoKeyTitle: Provider secrets are encrypted but no key is set
providers.encryptedNoKeyDescription: The secrets file at {path} is SOPS-encrypted, but neither SOPS_AGE_KEY nor SOPS_AGE_KEY_FILE is set in .env. Set one to decrypt, or save a new API key below to overwrite the encrypted file.
providers.imageGenerationModeHelp: How this image model is invoked. `images-api` uses the standard /v1/images/generations endpoint (FLUX, Imagen; edit mode via /v1/images/edits). `chat-multimodal` uses /v1/chat/completions with image content parts (Nano Banana, GPT-Image; edit mode via image parts in the user message).
providers.modelIdRequired: All models must have an ID
providers.deleteConfirmNamed: Are you sure you want to delete the provider "{name}"? This action cannot be undone. All model configurations associated with this provider will be removed.
providers.byomGuidance: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint works here. Point the base URL to your self-hosted inference server (e.g. vLLM, Ollama, LocalAI) or a third-party gateway like OpenRouter to use models such as LLaMA, Mistral, Gemini, and more.

enrollment.title: Two-factor authentication
enrollment.description: Protect your account with a time-based code from an authenticator app.
enrollment.notEnabledHint: Two-factor authentication is not yet enabled on your account.
enrollment.enabledHint: Two-factor authentication is active on your account.
enrollment.requiredByOrg: Your organization requires two-factor authentication. Enrol now to continue.
enrollment.passwordPromptDescription: Confirm your password to start setting up two-factor authentication.
enrollment.disablePromptDescription: Confirm your password to turn off two-factor authentication.
enrollment.regeneratePromptDescription: Confirm your password. Generating new codes invalidates all previous backup codes.

setup.title: Set up two-factor authentication
setup.qrInstructions: Scan the QR code with an authenticator app (Google Authenticator, 1Password, Authy), then enter the 6-digit code below.
setup.manualEntry: Can't scan? Enter this secret manually:

backupCodes.title: Save your backup codes
backupCodes.description: Each code can be used once if you lose access to your authenticator app.
backupCodes.warningOnce: We will not show these codes again. Download or copy them now.

verify.title: Verify your identity
verify.description: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
verify.invalid: Invalid or expired code. Please try again.
verify.tooManyAttempts: Too many failed attempts. Try again later.
verify.backupCodeSuccess: Backup code used. Consider regenerating your backup codes in Settings.

lowBackupCodes.body: Generate a new batch so you can still recover your account if you lose your authenticator.

enroll.title: Two-factor required
enroll.description: Your organization requires two-factor authentication to continue.

grace.body: Your organization will require all members to use a second factor. Enrol now to avoid interruption.

admin.confirmDescription: This disables two-factor for {name} and ends all their active sessions. They will have to enrol again on their next sign-in.

errors.enableFailed: Failed to start two-factor setup. Please try again.
errors.regenerateFailed: Failed to generate new backup codes.
errors.invalidCode: Invalid or expired code. Please try again.

accessDenied.title: Access denied
accessDenied.automations: You need Admin or Developer permissions to access automations.
accessDenied.integrations: You need Admin or Developer permissions to access integrations settings.
accessDenied.apiKeys: You need Admin or Developer permissions to manage API keys.
accessDenied.organization: You need Admin permissions to access organization settings.
accessDenied.teams: You need Admin permissions to access team settings.
accessDenied.agents: You need Admin or Developer permissions to manage agents.
accessDenied.mcpServers: You need Admin or Developer permissions to manage MCP servers.
accessDenied.branding: You need Admin permissions to access branding settings.
accessDenied.knowledge: Your account does not have permission to access knowledge.
accessDenied.workspaceNotFound: The workspace you're looking for doesn't exist or may have been removed.
accessDenied.disabled: Your account has been disabled. Contact support for assistance.
accessDenied.noMembership: You are not a member of this workspace. Please contact an administrator for access.

default.title: The Sovereign AI Platform
default.description: Local AI models, agents, skills, and automations — on your own infrastructure, connected to your data

metadata.keywords: sovereign AI, local AI, self-hosted AI platform, AI agents, AI automations, on-premises AI, data sovereignty, private infrastructure, AI skills, local language models, enterprise AI, data privacy, agentic AI, workflow automation

chat.title: Chat
chat.description: Chat with AI to get help with your tasks.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage your account and organization settings.

personalization.title: Personalization
personalization.description: Help the assistant remember things about you across chats.

agents.title: Agents
agents.description: Create and manage AI agents for customer service.

mcpServers.title: MCP servers
mcpServers.description: Connect external tools via Model Context Protocol.

providers.title: Providers
providers.description: Manage LLM providers and their models.

login.title: Log in
login.description: Log in to your Tale account.

apiDocs.title: API documentation
apiDocs.description: Explore the Tale API with interactive documentation.

knowledge.title: Knowledge
knowledge.description: Manage your knowledge base for AI agents.

agent.title: Agent
agent.description: Configure and test your AI agent.

agentInstructions.title: Instructions
agentInstructions.description: Configure agent instructions and model settings.

agentKnowledge.title: Agent knowledge
agentKnowledge.description: Manage knowledge base access for your agent.

agentTools.title: Agent tools
agentTools.description: Select and configure tools available to your agent.

agentWebhook.title: Agent workers
agentWebhook.description: Manage worker URLs for your agent.

agentDelegation.title: Agent delegation
agentDelegation.description: Configure agent delegation to other agents.

automationTriggers.title: Triggers
automationTriggers.description: Manage automation triggers, schedules, and webhooks.

apiKeys.title: API keys
apiKeys.description: Create and manage API keys for integrations.

governance.title: Governance
governance.description: Manage governance policies and system prompts.

systemPrompt.title: System Prompt
systemPrompt.description: Set a mandatory system prompt that is applied to all agents and cannot be overridden by users.
systemPrompt.prefixDescription: This text is prepended before every agent's instructions and cannot be overridden.
systemPrompt.suffixDescription: This text is appended after every agent's instructions and cannot be overridden.

budgets.title: Budget rules
budgets.description: Set token, cost, and request limits per scope and billing period.
budgets.tokenLimitHelp: Total input + output tokens. 1M tokens ~ 750K words.
budgets.costLimitHelp: Hard spending cap in USD. GPT-4o ~ $10/1M tokens.
budgets.maxRequestsHelp: Total AI requests per period. Leave empty for no limit.
budgets.warningThresholdHelp: Alert when usage exceeds this percentage of the limit.
budgets.overrideHint: More specific scopes override broader ones: user > team > role > default. Org limits always apply as an additional cap.
budgets.noRulesDescription: Add a rule to start enforcing limits.
budgets.removeRuleConfirmDescription: This rule will no longer enforce budget limits. You can always re-create it later.

defaultModels.title: Default Models
defaultModels.description: Set default models for specific user groups or roles.
defaultModels.saved: Default model configuration has been updated
defaultModels.removeRuleConfirmDescription: This rule will no longer assign a default model to the matching users. You can always re-create it later.
defaultModels.noRulesDescription: Add a rule to set default models by scope.
defaultModels.allowlistConflictWarning: This model isn't currently permitted by the Model access allowlist for this scope. Users receiving this default will fall back to an allowlist-permitted model.
defaultModels.blocklistConflictWarning: This model is currently blocked by the Model access policy for this scope. Users receiving this default will fall back to a permitted model.

uploadPolicy.title: Upload policy
uploadPolicy.description: Configure allowed file types, maximum file size, and volume limits.

retentionPolicy.title: Retention policy
retentionPolicy.description: Configure how long each data type is kept before deletion.

drawer.title: Edit retention policy
drawer.description: Configure how long each data type is kept before deletion.

retentionPolicy.envDisabled: Retention is currently disabled by the operator (TALE_RETENTION_DISABLED=true). Cleanup will not run until the env flag is removed.

diff.title: Confirm retention changes
diff.description: Review the changes before saving. Cleanup applies the new policy on its next run.

deletionGrace.label: Grace period (days)
deletionGrace.helper: 0 = Pass A immediately hard-deletes (no trash window). >0 keeps rows visible in admin Trash for that many days before Pass B physically removes them.

boundsProposal.title: Operator has proposed retention bound changes.
boundsProposal.firstApplyTitle: Operator retention bounds need your initial approval.
boundsProposal.summary: {tightened} of {total} change(s) tighten retention. Review before applying.
boundsProposal.firstApplyDescription: No bounds have been applied for this organization yet. Review the operator config and Apply to start enforcing retention.
boundsProposal.detailsDescription: Review the operator-proposed retention bound changes before applying or rejecting.
boundsProposal.appliedToast: Bounds proposal applied. Cleanup will use new bounds on next run.
boundsProposal.rejectedToast: Bounds proposal rejected. Cleanup continues with previous bounds.

promptTemplates.title: Prompt templates
promptTemplates.description: Saved prompts across all visibility scopes (personal, team, global).

messageFeedback.title: Message feedback
messageFeedback.description: Thumbs/comments left on agent messages. Free-text comments may contain quoted user content.

memoryAudit.title: Memory audit log
memoryAudit.description: Change-log of personalization memories (proposed, approved, edited, dismissed, deleted).

customers.title: Customers
customers.description: Per-org CRM records: name, email, address, locale, metadata.

vendors.title: Vendors
vendors.description: Per-org vendor records: name, email, phone, address, free-text notes.

externalConversations.title: External conversations
externalConversations.description: Top-level customer-channel inbox (email/chat integrations). Cascades to message bodies.

messageMetadata.title: Message metadata
messageMetadata.description: Per-message reasoning, full prompt context window, tool I/O. High-PII derived data; sweep follows the message's thread retention.

chatFilterEvents.title: Chat filter events
chatFilterEvents.description: Telemetry for content-filter triggers (PII, profanity, custom rules). Useful for tuning the filter; carries quoted message excerpts.
chatFilterEvents.helper: Filter-event telemetry rows older than this will be deleted.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: In-app notifications (mentions, agent results, system alerts). May reference content from chats, documents, or workflow runs.
notifications.helper: Notification rows older than this will be deleted.

chatHistory.title: Chat history
chatHistory.description: Automatically delete chat threads (conversations with agents) older than the retention period. Message contents in the agent component are archived. Deletion is irreversible.
chatHistory.helper: Chat threads older than this (by last-updated time) will be deleted.

documents.title: Document retention
documents.description: Automatically delete documents that exceed the retention period. Use with caution — deleted documents cannot be recovered.
documents.helper: Documents older than this will be deleted.

userTemp.title: User temporary file cleanup
userTemp.description: Automatically delete files uploaded by users but never saved to a document (e.g. chat attachments, aborted uploads).
userTemp.helper: Temporary files older than this will be deleted.

agentTemp.title: Agent temporary file cleanup
agentTemp.description: Automatically delete files generated by agents but never saved to a document (e.g. intermediate outputs, knowledge files).
agentTemp.helper: Temporary files older than this will be deleted.

workflowLogs.title: Workflow execution logs
workflowLogs.description: Automatically delete workflow execution records and trigger logs older than the retention period. Includes per-execution variable/output storage blobs.
workflowLogs.helper: Workflow runs older than this will be deleted (1–365 days).

auditLogs.title: Audit logs
auditLogs.description: Automatically delete audit log entries older than the retention period. Note: audit logs are immutable historical records used for compliance; keep retention long enough to satisfy your audit/compliance requirements.
auditLogs.helper: Audit log entries older than this will be deleted (30–365 days).

usageLedger.title: Usage ledger
usageLedger.description: Automatically delete token-usage ledger rows older than the retention period. Warning: shortening this retention truncates historical analytics (budgets/reports rely on ledger rows).
usageLedger.helper: Usage ledger rows older than this will be deleted (30–3650 days).

loginAttempts.title: Login attempts
loginAttempts.description: Automatically delete failed-login tracking records (loginAttempts, per-hour block counters) older than the retention period. Note: these tables are deployment-wide — the strictest (shortest) value across all orgs is used.
loginAttempts.helper: Login attempt records older than this will be deleted (7–365 days).

passwordPolicy.title: Password policy
passwordPolicy.description: Configure minimum password length, character-class requirements, and rotation.
passwordPolicy.minLengthHint: Minimum number of characters required in new passwords.
passwordPolicy.rotationDaysHint: Users will be required to change their password after this many days. The grace window starts when rotation is first enabled, so existing users are not forced to change immediately.
passwordPolicy.invalidMinLength: Minimum length must be an integer between 6 and 128.
passwordPolicy.invalidRotationDays: Rotation period must be an integer between 1 and 3650.

twoFactorPolicy.title: Two-factor authentication
twoFactorPolicy.description: Require all password-authenticated users to enrol a second factor (TOTP) after a grace window.
twoFactorPolicy.policyDisabledHint: Two-factor authentication is optional for all users while this policy is disabled.
twoFactorPolicy.gracePeriodDaysHint: Days each user has from the first sign-in where enforcement applies to them before enrolment is required. Set to 0 for immediate enforcement.
twoFactorPolicy.exemptSsoUsersHint: Users whose only account is a federated identity (e.g. Microsoft Entra ID) rely on the upstream IdP for MFA.
twoFactorPolicy.invalidGrace: Grace period must be an integer between 0 and 30.

loginPolicy.title: Login attempt limits
loginPolicy.description: Lock accounts after repeated sign-in failures and grow the wait between attempts.
loginPolicy.maxAttemptsHint: Number of consecutive wrong-password attempts before the account is temporarily locked.
loginPolicy.backoffScheduleHint: Comma-separated wait times applied after each lockout, in seconds. The last entry is reused for further failures. Default: 1, 10, 60, 600.
loginPolicy.trustedProxiesHint: Comma-separated list of IPs / CIDRs / keywords (loopback, uniquelocal, linklocal) for reverse proxies sitting in front of this deployment. Used to recover the real client IP from X-Forwarded-For. Default: loopback, uniquelocal.
loginPolicy.invalidAttempts: Failure threshold must be an integer between 1 and 50.
loginPolicy.invalidSchedule: Backoff schedule must be a comma-separated list of non-negative numbers.
loginPolicy.invalidProxies: Trusted proxies must be a comma-separated list of IPs, CIDRs, or keywords (max 32).

featureFlags.title: Feature controls
featureFlags.description: Enable or disable features per user, team, or role.
featureFlags.maxContextTokensHint: Maximum number of context tokens for AI responses. Leave empty for no limit.
featureFlags.removeRuleConfirmDescription: This rule will no longer apply to the matching users. You can always re-create it later.
featureFlags.noRulesDescription: Add a rule to manage features per user, team, or role.

pii.title: PII protection
pii.description: Detect and mask or block personal data before sending to AI models.
pii.patternsDescription: Select which types of personal data to detect.
pii.customPatternsDescription: Add custom regex patterns to detect additional types of personal data.
pii.testAreaDescription: Paste sample text to verify which patterns are detected.
pii.testInputPlaceholder: Paste text here to test PII detection...
pii.testNoResults: No PII detected in the test input.

guardrailsOverview.title: Guardrails overview
guardrailsOverview.description: Read-only snapshot of the three filter layers. Each one runs in order per message: chat_filter → PII → moderation_provider.

contentSafety.title: Content safety
contentSafety.description: Word lists and admin-supplied regex patterns.
contentSafety.disabled: Disabled — add a category and enable to start filtering.

pii.title: PII detection
pii.description: Built-in patterns for emails, phones, IDs, etc.
pii.disabled: Disabled — PII detection is off for this organization.

moderation.title: Moderation provider
moderation.disabled: Disabled — no external moderation API configured.

recentEvents.title: Recent events
recentEvents.description: Last 50 detections / blocks / provider errors for this org. Raw matched text is never stored.
recentEvents.empty: No events yet. Detections from flag / mask / block modes will appear here once chat traffic starts flowing.

eventDetails.title: Event details
eventDetails.description: Full metadata for a guardrails event. Raw matched text is never stored.
eventDetails.truncatedValue: Yes — message exceeded the scan-length cap

contentSafety.title: Content safety
contentSafety.description: Word lists and custom regex applied to chat messages. All three modes (block / mask / flag) run in order and aggregate to a single audit event per message.
contentSafety.cannotManage: You need admin permissions to edit content safety policy.
contentSafety.applyToDescription: Input filters user messages before the model. Output filters the assistant's reply; may add per-chunk latency.
contentSafety.preferNonStreaming: Prefer non-streaming when output filter is on
contentSafety.preferNonStreamingDescription: Wait for the complete response before showing anything. Eliminates any brief flash of unfiltered content in exchange for higher perceived latency.
contentSafety.categoriesDescription: Each category has its own mode. block wins over mask wins over flag when multiple match.
contentSafety.categoriesEmpty: No categories configured. Add one to start filtering.
contentSafety.categorySheetDescription: Configure a content safety category, its enforcement mode, and its word list.
contentSafety.categoryEditorDescription: Block wins over mask wins over flag when multiple categories match the same message.
contentSafety.categoryModeDescription: Block refuses the message. Mask replaces matches with the configured placeholder. Flag records the detection but lets the message pass.
contentSafety.wordsDescription: One word or phrase per line. Case-insensitive. For CJK/Thai/Lao text, substring matching is used automatically; other scripts use Unicode-aware word boundaries.
contentSafety.deleteConfirm: Delete category "{label}"? This removes its {words} words and {patterns} patterns.
contentSafety.importReplaceConfirm: Replace {existing} existing words with {incoming} words from "{filename}"?

moderationProvider.title: Moderation provider
moderationProvider.description: Send chat messages to an external classifier (OpenAI Moderation, Azure Content Safety, Perspective, or any custom HTTPS endpoint). Paste the API key in the 'API key' section below — it's AES-encrypted server-side — and reference it in any header value as `{secretPlaceholder}`.
moderationProvider.cannotManage: You need admin permissions to configure the moderation provider.
moderationProvider.failBehaviorDescription: What to do when the provider times out, errors, or the circuit breaker is open. Input default is fail-open (let the message through); output default is fail-closed (block the response) since unreviewed model output has higher liability.
moderationProvider.providerDescription: Pick a built-in preset — the URL, headers, request template, and response parser are filled in for you. The API key itself is entered (and encrypted) in the API key section below, never in the policy config. Pick Custom JSONPath for any other HTTPS moderation API you want to plug in.
moderationProvider.presetAppliedMappings: Added {count} default category mappings (all in flag mode — tune them below).
moderationProvider.customJsonPathHint: Open Endpoint below, fill in your HTTPS URL, request body template, and the JSONPath for categories. The switch won't persist until those fields are set.
moderationProvider.apiKeyDescription: Pasted here, encrypted with AES-256-GCM on the server, and stored in governanceSecrets. The plaintext value replaces `{secretPlaceholder}` in your endpoint headers at request time. Use the full header value for Authorization-style headers (e.g. 'Bearer sk-…'); raw key for Azure's Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key.
moderationProvider.apiKeyPlaceholder: Bearer sk-... or raw API key
moderationProvider.endpointDescription: HTTPS URL, headers, request template, and timeout for the external call.
moderationProvider.editEndpointDescription: URL, headers, and request template are validated server-side. {textPlaceholder} and {directionPlaceholder} placeholders are JSON-safe; {secretPlaceholder} is only allowed in header values.
moderationProvider.endpointUrlFieldDescription: Full HTTPS URL of the moderation API (HTTP allowed for internal / localhost mocks). Only this host is contacted — redirects to other hosts are rejected for SSRF safety.
moderationProvider.requestTemplateDescription: JSON with {textPlaceholder} and optional {directionPlaceholder} placeholders. Substitution is JSON-safe; secrets are NOT permitted in the body, only in headers.
moderationProvider.parseResponseDescription: Tell us how the provider's JSON response looks so we can pull out the flagged categories. If you're not sure, run the provider once in a terminal (curl) and paste the response shape into your picks below.
moderationProvider.shapeStepDescription: Which format does the provider use for its category flags?
moderationProvider.pathStepDescription: Where the shape above lives inside the response. A dollar sign is the root; dots traverse objects; numbers in brackets index arrays.
moderationProvider.pathExampleTitle: Example response body this path matches:
moderationProvider.pathExampleExplanation: The path picks out the bold part.
moderationProvider.flaggedPathDescription: Some providers also return a single top-level boolean — e.g. OpenAI's $.results[0].flagged. If set, `false` here short-circuits the category check and the message passes. Leave empty for providers that don't have this field.
moderationProvider.categoryMappingsDescription: Map provider categories (e.g. OpenAI's `hate/threatening`) to an internal label, enforcement mode, and optional score threshold. Block wins over mask wins over flag.
moderationProvider.mappingsEmpty: No category mappings configured. Add one to enforce this provider's verdicts.
moderationProvider.mappingsWarning: No category mappings configured. The provider is being called on every message but nothing will ever be flagged, masked, or blocked — only mapped categories produce events. Add at least one mapping below, or re-apply a preset to seed defaults.
moderationProvider.deleteMappingConfirmDescription: This mapping will no longer apply when the provider returns this category.
moderationProvider.mappingDialogDescription: Block wins over mask wins over flag when multiple mappings fire.
moderationProvider.providerCategoryDescription: The exact category key the provider returns (e.g. `hate`, `hate/threatening`, `violence`).
moderationProvider.internalLabelDescription: Human-readable name shown in the audit log and dashboards.
moderationProvider.scoreThresholdDescription: Optional. When the provider returns scores, the mapping only fires when the category's score is at or above this value.
moderationProvider.testConnectionDescription: Send a sample message through the real provider path. Verifies the API key, endpoint URL, request template, response parser, and category mappings in one round-trip. No chat message is saved — this call bypasses the thread and audit pipeline.
moderationProvider.testPlaceholder: Type a sample message to send through the provider…
moderationProvider.testDefaultText: I want to kill everyone in this building
moderationProvider.presetAzureNote: Replace the subdomain with your own Azure resource name.
moderationProvider.presetPerspectiveNote: Perspective authenticates via a URL query parameter, not a header. After applying the preset, open Endpoint and append ?key=YOUR_PERSPECTIVE_KEY to the URL. (The encrypted API-key field below is not used by Perspective.)

trash.title: Trash
trash.description: Recover retention-trashed records before they are permanently deleted at the end of the grace window.
trash.empty: Nothing in the trash. Retention will move expired rows here once their grace window starts.

restore.label: Restore
restore.trashedDescription: Bring this record back to active. It will return to its source list.
restore.expiredDescription: This record was deleted by retention policy. Restoring overrides that policy and brings the record back to active. Audited.

activeHolds.title: Active holds
activeHolds.description: Entities currently protected from cleanup and deletion. Place holds at the user (custodian) or organization level.

empty.title: No active legal holds
empty.description: Place a custodian hold on a specific user, or an organization-wide hold from the action above.

releaseRequests.title: Release requests
releaseRequests.description: Pending requests need a different admin to approve. Approved requests still wait out the cooldown before they take effect.

empty.title: No release requests
empty.description: Filed release requests appear here for dual-control review.

matters.title: Matters
matters.description: Group holds by case. Closing a matter files release requests for every linked hold (still subject to dual-control approval).

empty.title: No matters
empty.description: Create a matter to group holds by case number or eDiscovery scope.

history.title: Release history
history.description: Read-only audit of effected and rejected release requests.

empty.title: No completed release requests yet
empty.description: Effected and rejected releases appear here.

placeHold.title: Place legal hold
placeHold.description: Preserve content for litigation, audit, or eDiscovery. Place a custodian hold on a specific user, or an organization-wide hold. A reason is required and recorded in the audit log.
placeHold.orgWarning: Organization-wide holds freeze ALL retention for every entity in this tenant. This is a deliberate, audit-logged action — review the matter context before continuing.
placeHold.orgConfirmDescription: Required for organization-wide holds. The submit button stays disabled until the phrase matches exactly.
placeHold.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Litigation hold for Acme v. Co. (matter #2026-04)

requestRelease.title: Request release
requestRelease.description: Filing a release request kicks off a dual-control flow. A different admin must approve, and the release does not take effect until after the cooldown.

approveRelease.title: Approve release
approveRelease.description: Approving this request will release the hold after the configured cooldown. This is the second of two admin signatures.
approveRelease.selfApproveBlocked: A different admin must approve. Self-approve is blocked unless the deployment is configured for single-admin mode.

rejectRelease.title: Reject release
rejectRelease.description: Rejecting closes the request without releasing the hold.

upsertMatter.description: Group related holds under one matter. Closing the matter later files release requests for every linked hold.

closeMatter.title: Close matter
closeMatter.description: Closing this matter will file release requests for {count} active linked hold(s). Each release still requires dual-control approval before it takes effect.
closeMatter.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Matter closed; preservation no longer required.

toasts.placedDescription: The {targetType} is now protected from cleanup and deletion.
toasts.releaseRequestedDescription: Awaiting approval from a different admin.

generic.title: Something went wrong
generic.description: Please try again. If the problem persists, contact your administrator.

errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to do this.
errors.alreadyActive: An active legal hold already exists on this target.
errors.matterNotFound: That matter does not exist in this organization.
errors.targetNotInOrg: That target does not belong to this organization.
errors.targetOrgMismatch: Org-scoped hold target must equal the organization itself.
errors.alreadyReleased: This legal hold has already been released.
errors.releaseAlreadyPending: A release request is already pending for this hold.
errors.releaseNotPending: Release request is no longer in pending state.
errors.selfApprovalBlocked: A different admin must approve this release. Set TALE_LEGAL_HOLD_SINGLE_ADMIN_OK=true to allow self-approval in single-admin deployments.
errors.requesterNoLongerAdmin: The original requester is no longer an admin of this org. Reject this request and have a current admin file a new one.

modelAccess.title: Model access
modelAccess.description: Control which AI models are available to users, teams, and roles.
modelAccess.removeRuleConfirmDescription: This rule will no longer apply to the matching users. You can always re-create it later.
modelAccess.noRulesDescription: All models are available to all users.
modelAccess.saved: Model access policy has been updated
modelAccess.saveFailed: Failed to save model access policy
modelAccess.removeDefaultConfirmTitle: Remove permission for active default model?
modelAccess.removeDefaultConfirmBody: The following default model rule(s) currently assign models that would no longer be permitted: {rules}. Affected users will fall back to another permitted model.

dataSubjectRequests.title: Data subject requests
dataSubjectRequests.description: GDPR Art. 17 erasure receipts. File new requests, monitor SLA deadlines, and grant Art. 12(3) extensions when complex cases need more time.

empty.title: No erasure requests yet
empty.description: File a request from the action above to record an Art. 17 receipt.

status.pendingCoolingOff: Pending · {hours, plural, one {# h} other {# h}} until execution

errorState.title: Could not load this request
errorState.description: The receipt may have been deleted, or you may not have access to it.

coolingOffBanner.title: Cooling-off in progress
coolingOffBanner.description: This request is held in a cooling-off window before the cascade runs. Any admin can cancel it before the deadline elapses.

drawer.fullBreakdownTitle: Full breakdown across all data categories
drawer.fullBreakdownEntry: {rows, plural, =0 {0 erased} one {# erased} other {# erased}}{skippedByHold, plural, =0 {} other { · # skipped by hold}}
drawer.fullBreakdownNoData: No data in {count, plural, one {# other category} other {# other categories}}.

legalHoldBlock.title: Erasure blocked by legal hold
legalHoldBlock.orgHeld: An organization-wide hold is preserving every entity in this tenant. Release it before retrying erasure.
legalHoldBlock.userCustodianHeld: The subject is on a custodian hold. Release it before retrying erasure.
legalHoldBlock.generic: An active legal hold is preserving the subject's data.
legalHoldBlock.threadsCount: {count, plural, =1 {# held thread} other {# held threads}} preserved.
legalHoldBlock.documentsCount: {count, plural, =1 {# held document} other {# held documents}} preserved.

fileRequest.title: File erasure request
fileRequest.description: Records an Art. 17 receipt for the chosen subject and runs the cascade asynchronously. Identity verification is the filing admin's responsibility — confirm the subject's identity through your organization's process before continuing.
fileRequest.reasonCodeDescription: GDPR Art. 17(1)(a)–(f) plus the operational contract-termination ground.
fileRequest.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Subject withdrew consent on 2026-05-08 — HR ticket #4711
fileRequest.confirmDescription: Required for every erasure request. The submit button stays disabled until the phrase matches exactly.

extendDeadline.title: Extend Art. 12(3) deadline
extendDeadline.description: Add up to 60 days to the response window. The extension itself must be communicated to the subject within the original month, with reasons, per Art. 12(3). Each request can be extended at most once.
extendDeadline.extraDaysDescription: Whole days added on top of the original 30-day deadline.
extendDeadline.extraDaysError: Enter a whole number between {min} and {max}.
extendDeadline.extensionReasonPlaceholder: e.g. Cross-border legal review — counsel asked for an extra two weeks

retry.title: Retry erasure
retry.description: Re-runs the cascade for this request. Use after releasing a blocking legal hold or after fixing whatever caused the previous failure.

cancel.title: Cancel erasure request
cancel.description: Aborts this request before it executes. The receipt row is preserved as a record of the cancellation, but no data is deleted. Cancellation is only possible during the cooling-off window.
cancel.reasonPlaceholder: e.g. Subject withdrew their request — verified via HR ticket #4712

toasts.filedDescription: The cascade is running. Open the receipt to track progress.
toasts.extendedDescription: Added {days, plural, =1 {# day} other {# days}} to the response window.
toasts.cancelledDescription: The request will not execute. The receipt row is preserved for the audit trail.

generic.title: Something went wrong
generic.description: Please try again. If the problem persists, contact your administrator.

errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to do this.
errors.alreadyPending: An erasure request for this subject is already pending or running.
errors.legalHoldBlocked: An active legal hold is preserving this subject's data.
errors.notRetriable: This request is not in a state that can be retried.
errors.notExtendable: This request is in a terminal state and cannot be extended.
errors.alreadyExtended: This request has already been extended once. Art. 12(3) allows a single extension.
errors.deadlineLapsed: The original Art. 12(3) deadline has already passed; extensions cannot be granted retroactively.
errors.reasonTooShort: Provide a reason of at least 10 characters.
errors.notCancellable: Only pending requests in the cooling-off window can be cancelled.
errors.cannotCancelAfterCooldown: The cooling-off window has elapsed; the processor has already been dispatched.
errors.rateLimited: You have reached the daily erasure-request limit for your account. Try again later.
errors.dualApprovalRequired: Dual approval is required: the filer of an erasure request cannot also approve it.

dsarPolicy.title: Data subject request governance
dsarPolicy.description: Cooling-off window, dual-admin approval, and per-admin daily filing limit for GDPR Art. 17 erasure requests.

coolingOffHours.label: Cooling-off window (hours)
coolingOffHours.description: Hours between filing a request and the cascade actually running. Any admin can cancel within this window. Set to 0 to execute immediately. Range: 0–72.

requireDualApproval.label: Require dual approval
requireDualApproval.description: When enabled, every filed request must be approved by a second admin (the filer cannot approve their own request) before the cooling-off window starts. Recommended for high-stakes deployments with two or more admins.

dailyLimitPerAdmin.label: Daily limit per admin
dailyLimitPerAdmin.description: Maximum number of erasure requests a single admin may file per day. Caps blast radius from a compromised admin credential. Range: 1–50.

dsarPolicy.saveFailed: Could not save the governance policy. Please try again.
dsarPolicy.invalidCoolingOffHours: Cooling-off window must be a whole number between 0 and 72.
dsarPolicy.invalidDailyLimit: Daily limit must be a whole number between 1 and 50.
dsarPolicy.ownerOnlyNotice: Only the org owner can change this policy. Admins can read it. To weaken any safeguard (shorter cooling-off, disabling dual approval, or raising the daily limit), the change is staged for 24 hours so any admin can revert it.
dsarPolicy.loosenProposedDescription: Weakening changes apply after a 24-hour grace window. Any admin can cancel before then.
dsarPolicy.pendingFieldLocked: Locked while review window is open

pendingBanner.title: Pending policy weakening
pendingBanner.description: Proposed by {name}. Any admin can cancel before this takes effect.

library.title: Prompt library
library.description: Browse, save, and share prompt templates.

emptyState.title: No prompts yet
emptyState.description: Create your first prompt to save and reuse it across conversations
emptyState.filteredDescription: Try clearing your search or filters.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete prompt
deleteConfirm.description: Are you sure you want to delete "{title}"? This action cannot be undone.

form.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of what this prompt does...
form.versionConflictDescription: Someone saved v{version} while you were editing. Load the latest to re-anchor before saving.
form.versionConflictDirtyWarning: Your unsaved edits will be discarded.
form.bytesOverLimitAlert: Content exceeds the size limit. Shorten the prompt to enable Save.

toast.versionConflict: Newer version detected — load the latest before saving
toast.restoreStale: Version history changed — refresh to retry
toast.rateLimited: Saving too quickly — wait a moment before trying again
toast.forbidden: You don't have permission for this prompt
toast.tooLarge: A field exceeds the allowed length

history.dialogDescription: Compare and restore past versions of this prompt.
history.restoreConfirmDescription: Restore content from v{source} as v{target}. Existing versions are preserved.
history.keyboardHint: Enter compares · R or Shift+Enter restores

tagsInput.tooLong: Tag must be {max} characters or fewer

mcpServers.title: MCP Servers
mcpServers.description: Connect to internal data sources and tools via the Model Context Protocol.
mcpServers.deleteConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this MCP server connection?
mcpServers.emptyDescription: Add an MCP server to connect external tools and data sources to your agents.

tools.title: Discovered Tools
tools.noTools: No tools discovered. Test the connection first.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.dsarScheduledBody: An erasure request was filed for a subject in this org. It will execute in {coolingOffHours}h. Open the receipt to review or cancel.
notifications.dsarApprovalNeededBody: An erasure request was filed and requires a second admin's approval before it can run.
notifications.dsarCancelledBody: A pending erasure request was cancelled before its cooling-off window elapsed.
notifications.dsarPolicyTightenedBody: The DSAR governance policy was changed to a stricter setting. Stricter changes apply immediately.
notifications.dsarPolicyLoosenProposedBody: A weakening of the DSAR governance policy was proposed. Any admin can cancel before the 24-hour grace window elapses.
notifications.dsarPolicyLoosenAppliedBody: The proposed weakening of the DSAR governance policy took effect after the 24-hour grace window.
notifications.dsarPolicyLoosenCancelledBody: The pending weakening of the DSAR governance policy was cancelled before it took effect.

usage.title: Usage Analytics
usage.description: Token consumption, requests, and active users over time.
usage.cappedNotice: Results are capped because the time range contains too many records. Narrow the range for complete data.
usage.activeUsersTooltip: Users who made AI requests during this period.

empty.title: No usage yet
empty.description: No AI usage has been recorded for the selected period.

emptyModels.title: No model usage yet
emptyModels.description: No LLM calls have been recorded for the selected period. Integration and transcription usage is shown under Top Assistants.

feedback.title: Feedback Analytics
feedback.description: User-submitted thumbs up/down and arena verdicts on assistant responses.

errors.unauthorized: You don't have access to this page

cards.sentimentCapped: Sentiment unavailable while results are partial

topAgents.title: Top Assistants by feedback
topAgents.emptyDescription: Per-assistant breakdown appears here once users rate assistant replies.

topModels.title: Top Models by feedback
topModels.emptyDescription: Per-model breakdown appears here once users rate replies whose model is captured.

topMatchups.title: Top Model Matchups
topMatchups.emptyDescription: Per-pair head-to-head wins appear here once users compare two models in arena mode.

empty.title: No feedback collected yet
empty.description: When users tap thumbs up/down on a chat reply or pick a winner in arena mode, it shows up here.

filterEmpty.title: No feedback matches your filters
filterEmpty.description: Try removing or relaxing the active filters.

cappedNotice.title: Showing partial results
cappedNotice.description: Your organization has more than 50,000 entries in the selected window. Narrow the window for accurate counts.

recent.title: Recent feedback
recent.emptyDescription: Adjust the period or filters to see more.

toast.title: Upgraded to v{version}
toast.description: See what changed in this release.

page.title: Personalization
page.description: Help the assistant remember things about you across chats. Off by default — enable below.

orgDefault.label: Organization default (admin)
orgDefault.description: When on, every member of this organization has personalization enabled by default. Members who explicitly opt out keep their choice.

enable.label: Enable personalization
enable.description: When on, the assistant uses your custom instructions and saved memories. Turn off any time to stop injection.

customInstructions.label: Custom instructions
customInstructions.description: Things you want the assistant to keep in mind in every reply. Max 4000 characters.
customInstructions.placeholder: e.g. Reply concisely. Default to TypeScript when discussing code.

memories.title: Saved memories
memories.empty: Memories appear here after the assistant proposes them and you save.
memories.deleteConfirm: Delete this memory? This can't be undone.

dataNotice.default: AI can make mistakes—verify responses and do not share sensitive data.
